cases.observation: Official SDK 1.29.0 completed stdio initialize, discovery, call, and shutdown

unmatched response ID detected
unsupported negotiated protocol detected
tools/list response arrived without negotiated tools capability
malformed required keyword rejected
schema nesting exceeded the 64-level registration budget
instruction-shaped server metadata classified as untrusted
instruction-shaped tool description classified as untrusted
131072-byte tool result exceeded the 65536-byte boundary
credential canary detected before render/log/model use
initialization deadline fired and child was terminated
hard deadline emitted cancellation for the exact request ID
shutdown escalated stdin close → SIGTERM → SIGKILL on bounded deadlines

SSE event ID namespace did not match the negotiated session
authorization challenge pointed to a different resource origin; token forwarding denied
transport abort was followed by explicit cancellation for the exact request ID

invalid Origin rejected before initialization
missing client credential rejected with 401
revoked client credential rejected before session use
incomplete Accept negotiation rejected
non-JSON request content rejected
invalid JSON returned bounded parse error
JSON-RPC batch rejected for pinned revision
post-initialize request without protocol header rejected
unknown/mismatched session rejected
accepted notification returned empty HTTP 202

shared contract verifier passed the referenced Bridge invariant
reject: sensitivity_scope

limitations: Role ownership is assigned, but real people and signoff are intentionally not fabricated.
The Ground runner proves a pinned SDK baseline and raw hostile boundaries; it is not the production Connect or Bridge runtime.
Schema depth is executable, while complete node-count, regex-complexity, and recursive-reference budgets still require the production schema compiler.
Real provider OAuth, remote network faults, production credential brokerage, and production Tasks interoperability remain later-stratum evidence.
